heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 19 November 2025 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 5.3°C), outside temperature (down 5.3°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.3°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 2573 lux), cloud levels (down 112 m), inside temperature (up 9.3°C), and inside humidity (up 8%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 97.0m lower than yesterday, 112.2m higher than the past week, 130.5m higher than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 623m and a peak of 1932m, averaging 1159.0m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; on the cooler side. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, little or no wind. Possible light showers, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and weak sunlight because of clouds. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-2.3°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
